steps.title: Identify the main purpose of the text
steps.content: The text introduces Chen and colleagues' study, then pivots with "However" to emphasize a concern about that study: its small sample size may increase the potential for biased analysis and exaggerated effects. The main purpose is therefore to note a potential concern about the findings of a scientific study.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — To note a potential concern about the findings of a scientific study: Matches the text's main purpose — it introduces Chen et al.'s study and then explains why its findings may be overstated. ✓
  • Choice B — To explain how the behavior of a fish species has changed over time: Misreads the text as describing behavioral change in the species itself rather than questioning the study's methodology. ✗
  • Choice C — To discuss an aspect of ocean acidification that is frequently overlooked: Confuses ocean acidification as a subject of analysis rather than a variable in the study's execution. ✗
  • Choice D — To present a debate between two research teams about a cause of ocean acidification: No such debate between teams appears in the passage. ✗
That is the correct answer.
